observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) Collecting
Officer to continuously comply with the policy on remittance of collections daily and
intact; and (b) Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the timely
preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements for fair presentation of the Cash in
Bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to
(a) maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards, Property Cards per category
of PPE and updated Property Acknowledgement Receipt for issuance to end-users to
reflect the correct balance of each PPE account; (b) investigate the causes of the
unreconciled difference of ₱3,493,747.16 and make the necessary adjustments in the
physical inventory report and the accounting records, as warranted; (c) derecognize
unserviceable properties from the accounting books and property records upon
disposal thereof based on the approved Inventory and Inspection Report on
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); (d) recognize in the accounting books and in the
property records the donated motor vehicles totaling ₱4,380,002.00; (e) apply with
the Land Transportation Office the registration and issuance of government plates
for the 27 motor vehicles; and (f) strictly observe the Guidelines and Procedures in
the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition
of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items,
for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Planning and
Development Council and the Implementing Offices to(i) pursue the implementation of
the PPAs based on their original timeline so as to attain their optimum value to the
constituents according to their respective purpose; and (ii) monitor the status of
project implementation to ensure the prompt and timely execution of PPAs; (b)
Municipal Accountant to (i) facilitate the submission of unsubmitted DVs; and (ii)
check the completeness of documents including the aforementioned approved CSHP
before effecting payment; and (c) BAC Secretariat to submit the duly approved CSHP
in consonance with Section 5.3 of DOLE DO No. 13 s. of 1998 and Section 37.2.3(f) of
RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the LDDRM Office to optimize the
use of the LDRRMF by fully implementing the planned programs, projects and
activities (PPAs) in the LDRRMFIP and monitor their implementation to strengthen
the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i)
require the complete documentation of disbursements prior to payment; and (ii)
submit immediately to the Audit Team the 22 unsubmitted disbursement vouchers
and their supporting documents.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant to
require the complete documentation of disbursements prior to payment; (b) MSWDO
and GSO to submit the report on the distribution list of welfare goods, Care Bags and
food packs; and (c) MHO and HRMO to submit the certification on the number of
frontliners and patients served with meals during the pandemic.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the withholding and full remittance
of the taxes withheld within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue
Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to comply with the full remittance of withheld employees’
premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in
compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to continue to comply with the regulations on withholding and
remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Point System,
Technical Working Group (TWG) and secretariat to fully implement GAD-related
PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for each PPA so that the desired
outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest extent by the intended
beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to require the persons liable to settle their accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and settle the differences in the balances of the Cash in Bank account which appeared in the general ledger and cashbook; (b) Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the depository bank and work on the completion and submission of the documentary requirements for the reactivation of dormant bank accounts; (c) concerned Municipal officials, upon the reactivation of the accounts, expedite the full implementation of the identified projects under the Trust Fund – BUB for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund without further delay; and (d) Municipal Treasurer to refund/return the unspent trust balances to concerned source agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) General Services Officer-OIC to (i) submit the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) pertaining to the textbooks and instructional materials distributed to Guiguinto District to facilitate the recording of the appropriate expense account by the Municipal Accountant for the dormant balance of ₱345,720.00;(ii) maintain stock cards to monitor the movement of inventories; (iii) use Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) as proof of distribution to requesting units/ department and summarize them in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issues (RSMI) and submit to the Accounting office on a monthly basis; (iv) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the reported Inventory balances in the books; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) taking up the issued/consumed supplies and materials for the dormant balance of ₱345,720.00 based on the RSMI for issued inventories; (ii) prepare the required subsidiary ledger cards to record acquisition, issuance and disposal of inventories; and (iii) recognize inventory and expense in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Inventories account at year-end.

We also recommended that the Municipal Administrator, when the budget permits, (i) consider the provision of a storage room for the procurement of supplies in significant quantities; and (ii) assign a storekeeper to effectively discharge property accountability and maintenance of inventories.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the necessary adjustments to reclassify the asset items classifiable as Machinery and Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures, Books and Other Property, Plant and Equipment and related accumulated depreciation to the appropriate accounts and thereafter, to observe the ₱15,000.00 capitalization threshold when classifying/recording these tangible items as PPE; (ii) recognize depreciation expenses on PPEs purchased in CY 2020; (iii) derecognize unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal thereof based on the approved Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); (iv) assess the economic value of the PPE as basis for the recognition of impairment loss; (v) derecognize missing/sold properties after verification of completeness of supporting documents; and (vi) provide the General Service Officer with the list of all insurable assets and insure all these properties with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 81-160 and 92-390.

We also recommended that the (b) General Service Officer (i) maintain complete and updated property cards per category of PPE and updated Property Acknowledgement Receipt for issuance to end-users; (ii) report all obsolete and unserviceable PPE in the IIRUP duly signed by the Head of Accounting Unit, Inspector/Appraisal Committee and approved by the LCE or authorized representative, and submit the report to the Disposal Committee for proper disposition; and (iii) keep the unserviceable PPE in a secure place or “bodega” and dispose them within a reasonable time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: (a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to monitor the status of implementation of projects to ensure the prompt and timely execution of PPAs; (b) Municipal Accountant to check the adequate documentation of transactions prior to payment and to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to avoid issuance of the notice of suspension for  the lacking supporting documents; (c) BAC secretariat to submit  the duly approved Construction Safety and Health Program (CSHP) to preclude the issuance of a notice of suspension; and (d) Municipal Accountant to require the approved CSHP prior to the processing of the contractor’s claim either for advance payment or first progress billing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) LDRRM Officer to optimize the use of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) by fully implementing the planned programs, projects and activities (PPAs) in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and to monitor their implementation to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters; and (b) Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to refrain from charging non-disaster related expenses against the fund and avoid payment for PPAs under the LDRRMF which are more appropriately chargeable against the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) implementing office of the BGCM to (i) prepare a monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format; (ii) comply with the posting and the reportorial requirements on monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format; (iii) post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month; and (iv) send a written notice to the regulatory and oversight agencies when said reports have been posted on its website; (b) Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to post the Notice of Award, Contract and Purchase Order in the website of the Procuring Entity (PE) or if none, any conspicuous place in the premises of the Procuring Entity and the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement; and (c) Municipal Accountant to be cautious in the use of account titles in the recording of transactions in the books and strictly observe the chart of account with description provided under the GAM for LGUs. Volume III.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the requirements provided in GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020 by (a) posting the procurement reports such as the Annual Procurement Plan (APPs), Procurement Monitoring Reports (PMRs) and Agency Procurement Compliance and Performance Indicator (APCPI) Results, Notice of Award, Contract and Purchase Order in the website of the Procuring Entity (PE) or if none, any conspicuous place in the premises of the Procuring Entity and the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement made within the set schedule in order to promote transparency in the procurement process; and (b) submitting to the GPPB the approved APPs, PMRs and APCPI Results through the electronic mail address.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to faithfully comply with the full remittance of the taxes withheld within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Project Monitoring Committee Team to give due priority in monitoring the implementation of GAD PPAs, evaluate and report the level and extent that GAD PPAs have become effective and efficient in checking GAD issues; and (b) GAD Focal Point System, Technical Working Group(TWG) and Secretariat to strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for said purposes so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest extent by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to require the persons named liable for the disallowed transactions to settle their accounts through payment.

Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to faithfully comply with the full

Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to invariably comply with the full remittance
of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following
month in compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

10.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to comply with the regulations on
withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the
prescribed period.

11.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with regulations
on the withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the
prescribed period.

12.18 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal
Project Monitoring Committee Team to give due priority in monitoring the
implementation of GAD PPAs, evaluate and report the level and extent that GAD
PPAs have become effective and efficient in checking GAD issues; and (b) GAD
Focal Point System, Technical Working Group(TWG) and Secretariat to strive for
the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set
aside for said purposes so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are
enjoyed to the fullest extent by the intended beneficiaries.

13.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to require the persons named liable for the
disallowed transactions to settle their accounts through payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MAO to (i) periodically reconcile records with the MTO to prevent the incurrence of stale checks in the custody of the latter; (ii) restore to the Cash in Bank account and recognize the appropriate Payable and/or Government Equity account  the 93 stale checks totaling ₱1,406,654.17; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the concerned payees at least one month before their checks  become stale as required under Section 59 of  Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and the MT to facilitate the closure of all dormant accounts and transfer the dormant cash in bank balances to an active account to enable the Municipal Government of Guiguinto to fund the delivery of basic services to its constituents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MAO to (a) strictly follow the prescribed accounting entries in the recording of the purchases and issuances of inventories particularly those purchased out of trust fund; and (b) account in the books the year-end inventory of medical supplies, drugs and medicines in the custody of the Property Supply Officer at the ONG for fair presentation of inventory accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO (a) and MAO to (i) investigate the discrepancy noted between the inventory report of PPE and the MAO’s property records; (ii) strengthen property management by performing a periodic inventory of PPE and reconciliation of records; and (iii) maintain PCs and PPELCs, respectively; (b) prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property supported with the necessary information and data in the columns provided therein for evaluation. In case where technical knowledge for the determination of the accurate condition, or fair value of the property to be inspected is needed, a COA Technical Inspector with the required knowledge and training shall inspect the same; (c) keep the unserviceable PPE in a secure place or “bodega” and disperse them within a reasonable time; and (d) to prepare and maintain property cards.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (a) henceforth, transmit immediately to the Audit Team all unsubmitted vouchers to prevent the suspension/disallowance of payments made; and (b) ensure that the submission of accounts is invariably complied within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to (a) strictly adhere to the provision that all payments should be made by check directly payable to the suppliers; (b) adopt public bidding as the primary mode of procurement; and (c) ensure that the proper procurement modality is implemented by the concerned department head responsible for the procurement.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” the DVs and their supporting documents after the checks are issued to the payees in strict compliance with Section 2(Q) of COA Circular No. 92-389 dated November 13, 1992.

We recommended that the LCE as head of the Local School Board to (a) instruct its members to discontinue the charging of ineligible expenses to SEF; and (b) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No, 7160, and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 9i, 2017 concerning the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure strict monitoring and supervision in the implementation of projects; (ii) make certain that the ABC and other documentary requirements are prepared and submitted pursuant to the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; (iii) require the concerned Contractor to cause the repair of the defective and damaged portion of the road project pursuant to the one year warranty period commencing from the date of project completion up to final acceptance, or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) impose liquidating damages against the contractor for the delay in the completion of the project, if warranted; and (ii)  ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer to provide full appropriation of not less than five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources for the LDRRMF to support disaster risk management activities; (b) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects, activities listed in the MDRRMP to ensure the full implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs; and (c) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to facilitate the transfer of the balances of the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the STF in observance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) to (i) properly plan the PPAs for inclusion in the AIP for 20% Development Plan  and refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature capital investments or those not essential or relevant to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes as identified under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 – 1 to assure the attainment of development objectives of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure the immediate completion of ongoing projects; and (ii) require the project engineers to perform regular and thorough monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that construction works are accomplished in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contracts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the: (a) Municipal Engineer to (i) require the respective contractors to repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; (ii) ensure that the infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.
We recommended that the LCE and the MENRO extend assistance in any form to barangays with no MRF for the attainment of common objectives on solid waste management.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Acting Municipal HRMO to (a) submit to the Audit Team the duly signed and approved Contract of Service of all JO personnel employed as of December 31, 2019; (b) avoid the hiring of JO personnel whose function overlap/duplicate those of the regular employees of the Municipality; (c) hire job order personnel based on necessity and skills/competencies which should be identified/attributed to specific local projects authorized by the Sanggunian for a period not exceeding six months; and (d) require the JO personnel to prepare and submit monthly accomplishment reports.

We recommended that the LCE, through its Focal Person (a) ensure complete and timely implementation of GAD PPAs in performing the periodic monitoring of significant dates and milestones in the implementation of PPAs so that timely interventions may be introduced for the substantial attainment of desired objectives and outputs; (b) submit a quarterly AR  to faithfully monitor the PPAs, and be able to conveniently prepare the consolidated annual AR; and (c) observe the deadline of submission of GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to faithfully comply with the full remittance of the taxes withheld within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to be compliant with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MAO to (i) periodically reconcile records with the MTO to prevent the incurrence of stale checks in the custody of the latter; (ii) restore to the Cash in Bank account and recognize the appropriate Payable and/or Government Equity account  the 93 stale checks totaling ₱1,406,654.17; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the concerned payees at least one month before their checks  become stale as required under Section 59 of  Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and the MT to facilitate the closure of all dormant accounts and transfer the dormant cash in bank balances to an active account to enable the Municipal Government of Guiguinto to fund the delivery of basic services to its constituents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” the DVs and their supporting documents after the checks are issued to the payees in strict compliance with Section 2(Q) of COA Circular No. 92-389 dated November 13, 1992.

We recommended that the LCE as head of the Local School Board to (a) instruct its members to discontinue the charging of ineligible expenses to SEF; and (b) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No, 7160, and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 9i, 2017 concerning the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure strict monitoring and supervision in the implementation of projects; (ii) make certain that the ABC and other documentary requirements are prepared and submitted pursuant to the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; (iii) require the concerned Contractor to cause the repair of the defective and damaged portion of the road project pursuant to the one year warranty period commencing from the date of project completion up to final acceptance, or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) impose liquidating damages against the contractor for the delay in the completion of the project, if warranted; and (ii)  ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.

10.18	We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer to provide full appropriation of not less than five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources for the LDRRMF to support disaster risk management activities; (b) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects, activities listed in the MDRRMP to ensure the full implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs; and (c) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to facilitate the transfer of the balances of the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the STF in observance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

11.15	We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) to (i) properly plan the PPAs for inclusion in the AIP for 20% Development Plan  and refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature capital investments or those not essential or relevant to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes as identified under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 – 1 to assure the attainment of development objectives of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure the immediate completion of ongoing projects; and (ii) require the project engineers to perform regular and thorough monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that construction works are accomplished in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contracts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the: (a) Municipal Engineer to (i) require the respective contractors to repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; (ii) ensure that the infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.
We recommended that the LCE and the MENRO extend assistance in any form to barangays with no MRF for the attainment of common objectives on solid waste management.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Acting Municipal HRMO to (a) submit to the Audit Team the duly signed and approved Contract of Service of all JO personnel employed as of December 31, 2019; (b) avoid the hiring of JO personnel whose function overlap/duplicate those of the regular employees of the Municipality; (c) hire job order personnel based on necessity and skills/competencies which should be identified/attributed to specific local projects authorized by the Sanggunian for a period not exceeding six months; and (d) require the JO personnel to prepare and submit monthly accomplishment reports.

We recommended that the LCE, through its Focal Person (a) ensure complete and timely implementation of GAD PPAs in performing the periodic monitoring of significant dates and milestones in the implementation of PPAs so that timely interventions may be introduced for the substantial attainment of desired objectives and outputs; (b) submit a quarterly AR  to faithfully monitor the PPAs, and be able to conveniently prepare the consolidated annual AR; and (c) observe the deadline of submission of GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to faithfully comply with the full remittance of the taxes withheld within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to be compliant with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the LCE require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory.

We 	recommended that Management (a) compel all AOS to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances for the current and prior years and cause the withholding of the salaries of those accountable officers who failed to submit the liquidation reports within the prescribed period; (b) strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances particularly those granted to the non-employees of the Municipality;  and (c) consider designating a disbursing officer for the SEF to monitor the accountability in the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the MAO to continue to exert best efforts by coordinating with the farmers to discuss the issues and concerns regarding their possible payment/return of the money to the Municipality; and (b) if the circumstances warrant, and after exhausting all possible remedies, gather and submit all available documents necessary for the filing of the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts to the COA Auditor’s Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO and Accounting offices to (a) maintain Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Card for land for easy reconciliation of the account; (b) exert efforts to secure the land titles for untitled lots purchased by the Municipality; (c) immediately request for disposal of unserviceable property to prevent further deterioration; and (d) recognize in the books the Disaster Equipment amounting to ₱310,461.00.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of P.D. 1445 by immediate providing the COA of sufficient office space and storage of the documents for audit in order to comply with the submission of accounts requirement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely interventions may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the programs;  (b) properly identify the PPAs constituting the balance of the 20% DF for re-appropriation;  and (c) provide the necessary Sangguniang Bayan Resolution for the unexpended balances of the fund at year end to finance the development projects identified in the Annual Investment Program of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (a) compute for the liquidated damages to be imposed to  the contractor equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of one (1) percent of the cost of the  unperformed  portion of the works  for every day of delay;  (b) require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 in all procurements;  and (c) prepare and submit the ABC as part of the supporting documents in the detailed engineering of the projects implemented and for implementation.

We recommended that the (a) MSWDO conduct actual validation of the  families and individuals in the affected barangays to come up with the victim profiles/database; (b) submit to MDRRMO the provision for data base generation of disaster victims and relief goods for inclusion in the AIP for DRRMF;  (c) MDRRMO prepare the monthly and annual report on the utilization of the LDRRMF and  involve the MSWDO and other concerned offices in the AIP  deliberation to include database generation, stockpile of relief goods,  and ensure appropriateness of procurement for disaster need.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account and prepare the corresponding JEV to correct the erroneous charging of accounts to come up with a reliable financial statement and; (b) concerned officials to refrain from using the DRRMF for transactions which are not disaster-related.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) continue to exert best efforts to be fully compliant with Solid Waste Management Program;  (b) instruct the Municipal Engineer and MENRO to hasten the construction of the remaining 10 MRFs in the Barangay to help in achieving waste reduction within the Municipality; (c) intensify the conduct of people assemblies/forums to discuss issues on proper segregation and disposal of solid wastes; and (d) coordinate with the Barangay officials to address the power supply problems in the operation of the newly acquired equipment for the use of MRF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) GFPS or the MPDO to cause the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities; and (b) establish and submit the details of accomplishment reports together with its supporting documents to validate the expenses charged to GAD fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to remit immediately all trust obligations to the respective national agency and Government Corporation concerned.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely necessary interventions may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the programs; (b) direct the other responsible officials concerned to address the issue that impede the full implementation of priority projects; and (c) continue to invest in high-impact or development projects included in the AIP of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to remit immediately all trust obligations to the respective national agency and Government Corporation concerned.
We recommended that the LCE require the immediate settlement/refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory.

We recommended that the Management (a) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO and Assessor to (i) maintain the appropriate subsidiary/ property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, for control purposes; (ii) perform reconciliation and comparison between the books and per actual inventory of the PPE account and between the general ledger balance and the list of properties; (iii) reconcile their records for all lots owned by the Municipality  and record  the necessary adjustments in the books, if any; and (b) require the concerned Municipal official to facilitate the  titling  of lots owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with the aforementioned regulation and require the Municipal Engineering Office and other officials concerned to disclose all transactions involving PPAs for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) and the Municipal District Supervisor of Schools as co-chairmen ensure that only the authorized priority expenditures identified under the SEF mandate are planned and programmed in the annual school board budget to make certain that the use of the Fund is in accordance with its top priorities; and (b) require the Municipal Budget Officer in collaboration with the Municipal Accountant to desist from charging non-priority expenditures against the SEF, and payment thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations in the utilization of the financial assistance under the LGSF –ADM Program to assure the attainment of its objective to alleviate the living condition of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations on disaster preparedness and mitigation to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities.

We recommended that Management (a) continue being compliant with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; (b) involve closely all barangay officials in the strict implementation of mandatory segregation of wastes at source; and (c) effect the revision in the ten-year Development Plan and submit the same with the proper authority.
We recommended that management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on the invitation of observers in all stages of the procurement process.
We recommended that Management continue to remit immediately all trust obligations to the respective national agency and government corporation concerned.

We recommended that the Management (a) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO and Assessor to (i) maintain the appropriate subsidiary/ property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, for control purposes; (ii) perform reconciliation and comparison between the books and per actual inventory of the PPE account and between the general ledger balance and the list of properties; (iii) reconcile their records for all lots owned by the Municipality  and record  the necessary adjustments in the books, if any; and (b) require the concerned Municipal official to facilitate the  titling  of lots owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely necessary interventions may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the programs; (b) direct the other responsible officials concerned to address the issue that impede the full implementation of priority projects; and (c) continue to invest in high-impact or development projects included in the AIP of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations in the utilization of the financial assistance under the LGSF –ADM Program to assure the attainment of its objective to alleviate the living condition of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations on disaster preparedness and mitigation to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities.

We recommended that Management (a) continue being compliant with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; (b) involve closely all barangay officials in the strict implementation of mandatory segregation of wastes at source; and (c) effect the revision in the ten-year Development Plan and submit the same with the proper authority.
We recommended that management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on the invitation of observers in all stages of the procurement process.
We recommended that Management continue to remit immediately all trust obligations to the respective national agency and government corporation concerned.
We recommended that the LCE require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory.

We recommended  that the Municipal Mayor: (a) direct the Accountant and the GSO to set up and maintain complete property, plant and equipment  ledger cards, property cards and schedules for the PPE to strengthen the controls in the handling, recording and custodial responsibility  for the PPE; (b) require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to maintain Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLCs) for each real property (land) bearing the TCT No., property description, location, mode of acquisition, acquisition cost, addition, and disposition; (c) provide appropriation  for the titling of land holdings of the Municipality;  (d) direct the Municipal Assessor to coordinate  with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) to get the copy of owner’s duplicate certificate on file with the LRA to be able to determine the ownership of  TCT Nos. 237194 and 237592 that were alleged to be missing; (e)  require the Rural Health Physician to report to the Municipal Accountant any receipt of medical equipment by the Rural Health Units and Barangay Health Stations; and (f) require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books all  properties acquired through donation.

In view of the deficiencies noted, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) desist from granting cash advances of SB Officials to the Vice Mayor and require the immediate liquidation of the accumulated additional cash advances by issuing demand letter to the accountable officer concerned; (b) limit the granting of cash advances to elected officials only for their official travelling expenses; (c) strictly enforce the prescribed settlement period and the liquidation of previous cash advances of AO before processing new or additional cash advances; and (d) see to it that cash advances are granted only to duly designated disbursing and bonded officers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to review the accountability of the four non-bonded accountable officer and submit their application for bond with the fidelity fund of the Bureau of Treasury   pursuant to the Treasury Circular 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO: (a) to issue and renew  the ARE every two years or every time there is an issuance of equipment either to its own personnel  and other national agency personnel assigned at the municipality;  (b) to use inventory tags for issued  PPE and Receipt of Transfer of Accountability  whenever there are transfers of accountability between incoming and outgoing officers; and (c) prepare Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) upon issuance of small tangible items covered by approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS).

We recommended that the: (a) Local School Board ensure that only the authorized priority expenditures identified under the SEF mandate are planned and programmed in the SEF Annual Budget; (b) Municipal Budget Officer desist from improperly charging non-priority expenditures against the SEF; and (c) Municipal Accountant ensure that payments of expenditures are in accordance with the top priorities of the SEF.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to properly plan the programs, projects and activities to be implemented by the Municipality based on its Annual Investment Program in order to avoid realignment of funds and cause delays in the implementation of projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to provide allocation of 1 % of internal revenue allotment for child protection and formulate, coordinate, monitor, and evaluate the implementation of development projects and activities that will lead to the successful implementation of the program.
We recommended that Municipal Mayor continue to being compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) provide adequate and realistic budget for its GAD Plan by including therein only programs/projects/activities that promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s rights and promote women’s activities; (b)  require the  Municipal Budget Officer and the GAD Focal Person to desist from charging  non-related  expenditures against the GAD Fund which did not partake of the nature of GAD programs, projects and activities; (c) establish sex disaggregated data to institutionalize and improve GAD planning and budgeting;  and (d) enact a GAD Code and implement a comprehensive and sustainable GAD program.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor: (a) continue being compliant with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; and (b) involve closely the remaining three barangay officials in the strict implementation of mandatory segregation of wastes at source.
We recommended that Municipal Mayor continue to remit immediately all trust obligations to the respective national agencies and government corporations concerned.

1.11		We recommended  that the Municipal Mayor: (a) direct the Accountant and the GSO to set up and maintain complete property, plant and equipment  ledger cards, property cards and schedules for the PPE to strengthen the controls in the handling, recording and custodial responsibility  for the PPE; (b) require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to maintain Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLCs) for each real property (land) bearing the TCT No., property description, location, mode of acquisition, acquisition cost, addition, and disposition; (c) provide appropriation  for the titling of land holdings of the Municipality;  (d) direct the Municipal Assessor to coordinate  with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) to get the copy of owner’s duplicate certificate on file with the LRA to be able to determine the ownership of  TCT Nos. 237194 and 237592 that were alleged to be missing; (e)  require the Rural Health Physician to report to the Municipal Accountant any receipt of medical equipment by the Rural Health Units and Barangay Health Stations; and (f) require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books all  properties acquired through donation.

2.6		In view of the deficiencies noted, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) desist from granting cash advances of SB Officials to the Vice Mayor and require the immediate liquidation of the accumulated additional cash advances by issuing demand letter to the accountable officer concerned; (b) limit the granting of cash advances to elected officials only for their official travelling expenses; (c) strictly enforce the prescribed settlement period and the liquidation of previous cash advances of AO before processing new or additional cash advances; and (d) see to it that cash advances are granted only to duly designated disbursing and bonded officers.

3.6     		We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to review the accountability of the four non-bonded accountable officer and submit their application for bond with the fidelity fund of the Bureau of Treasury   pursuant to the Treasury Circular 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

4.12    	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO: (a) to issue and renew  the ARE every two years or every time there is an issuance of equipment either to its own personnel  and other national agency personnel assigned at the municipality;  (b) to use inventory tags for issued  PPE and Receipt of Transfer of Accountability  whenever there are transfers of accountability between incoming and outgoing officers; and (c) prepare Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) upon issuance of small tangible items covered by approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS).

5.7        	We recommended that the: (a) Local School Board ensure that only the authorized priority expenditures identified under the SEF mandate are planned and programmed in the SEF Annual Budget; (b) Municipal Budget Officer desist from improperly charging non-priority expenditures against the SEF; and (c) Municipal Accountant ensure that payments of expenditures are in accordance with the top priorities of the SEF.

6.5		We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to properly plan the programs, projects and activities to be implemented by the Municipality based on its Annual Investment Program in order to avoid realignment of funds and cause delays in the implementation of projects.

7.6		We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to provide allocation of 1 % of internal revenue allotment for child protection and formulate, coordinate, monitor, and evaluate the implementation of development projects and activities that will lead to the successful implementation of the program.
8.6	We recommended that Municipal Mayor continue to being compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) provide adequate and realistic budget for its GAD Plan by including therein only programs/projects/activities that promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s rights and promote women’s activities; (b)  require the  Municipal Budget Officer and the GAD Focal Person to desist from charging  non-related  expenditures against the GAD Fund which did not partake of the nature of GAD programs, projects and activities; (c) establish sex disaggregated data to institutionalize and improve GAD planning and budgeting;  and (d) enact a GAD Code and implement a comprehensive and sustainable GAD program.

10.8	We recommended that Municipal Mayor: (a) continue being compliant with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; and (b) involve closely the remaining three barangay officials in the strict implementation of mandatory segregation of wastes at source.
11.9	We recommended that Municipal Mayor continue to remit immediately all trust obligations to the respective national agencies and government corporations concerned.

12.3 	We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Mayor (i) instruct the officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit; and ( 	ii) require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and (b) Municipal Accountant comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.

We recommended that Management (a) require the GSO to submit to the audit team the results of the actual inventory of PPE for CY 2015 which should be reconciled with the records of the Accounting Office; (b)  direct the Accountant and the GSO to set up and maintain property ledger cards, property cards and schedules for the PPE to strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE;  and (c)  require GSO to insure buildings and heavy equipment with the GSIS to provide adequate protection of the insurable assets.

We recommended that management (a) direct the Municipal Assessor to submit a complete Assessment Roll on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December  to the Municipal Treasurer; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to exert diligent efforts to prepare the certified list of taxpayers within the prescribed period; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to draw the journal entry voucher pertaining to the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list and prepare and maintain a complete and updated subsidiary ledgers and schedules on RPT/SET receivables to properly monitor the collection and monitoring of real estate tax delinquencies.

We recommended that Management (a) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities for which DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (b) request the Local School Board to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.

We recommended that Management (a) explain/justify the procurement of three elf dump trucks through direct purchasing; (b) adhere strictly to the provisions of IRR of R.A. 9184 to promote transparency and competitiveness and obtain the most advantageous price for the Municipality.

We recommended that (a) 	 the laws, rules, regulations and guidelines issued by the government should be strictly implemented, complied, and observed more particularly by the government officials; and (b) Management should direct the refund of the irregular claims by the concerned personnel and officials responsible in the disbursement made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a)  	require the Accountant and the Treasurer to stop the payment of annual dues to various organizations and associations not in conformity with R.A. No. 7160 for lack of legal basis; (b)     require the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating claims without the available appropriations for the purpose; otherwise, require the Sangguniang Bayan to issue resolution for the realignment/issuance of supplemental of budget;  (c) advise the Municipal Accountant to see to it that claims for the membership, dues, and contributions be substantiated with the necessary budget and approved plans and programs as basis for the amount to be paid; and (d) advise the Municipal Treasurer that payments for the purpose made to duly recognized leagues and federations under the law be acknowledged only with the prescribed government Accountable Form No. 51.

We recommended that Management (a) 		 stop the practice of engaging the consultancy services of permanently appointed employee in order to avoid payment of double compensation and avail of the necessary services needed from a consultant whose services is undivided without conflict with other government agency which has already engaged his services thru a permanent appointment; and (b)  Management should direct the refund of the irregular claims by the concerned personnel and officials responsible in the disbursement made.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) review the accountability of every accountable officer in order to properly determine the appropriate bond thereto pursuant to the Treasury Circular; and (b) require the petty cash custodians to maintain PCR to facilitate the determination of accountability.

We   recommended   that   Management  (a)  		formulate a concrete waste management plan in accordance with the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” or R.A. 9003 by setting guidelines and targets for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through waste reduction at source and waste minimization measures; (b) provide assistance and guidance to the ten remaining barangays for their compliance relative to the establishment of their own MRF; and (c) embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.
We recommended that management require the Local Disaster Risk Management Officer to prepare a LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management (a) 	prepare a comprehensive GAD Plan and accomplishment reports so that the programs, projects and activities could be comprehensively planned and the execution thereof may be monitored effectively and measured accordingly; and (b) provide at least 5% of the total budget appropriation for GAD as required under Item III of COA of Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment transactions and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
